# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for Azure Blob Storage to store application logs and backups. Configure for standard performance tier with default access settings. Skip lifecycle management policies for now to keep the template simple. This is for our logging infrastructure.
# Category: cloud_storage_security

{
 "type": "Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts",
  "apiVersion": "2019-06-01",
  "location": "[resourceGroup().location]", //this will automatically pick up the location of the resource group it's deployed in. If this was created in East US region, the resource group will be deployed to Central US.
 "name": "[variables('storageAccountName')]", 
 "sku":{
   "name":"Standard_LRS"
  },
 "kind":"Storage", // This is not required for blob storage but we want a general purpose cloud storage. It's just used to distinguish this resource from others that may also be deployed in the same subscription. 
 "properties": {
    "accessTier":"Hot",
    "supportsHttpsTrafficOnly":"false", //This is the setting that allows access over plain HTTP which won't work if you're going to allow non-HTTPS traffic (e.g., to your webapp). 
    }
}
